output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    TestObserver observer(observerName);
    TestObserver target(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status
    return {observer.wasChanged(), observer.getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {

}

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <set>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return bool Whether batch operations work correctly
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    try {
        // 1. Create observer nodes
        auto node1 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
        auto node2 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
        
        // 2. Get observer graph instance
        auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
        
        // 3. Create batch ID using std::unique_ptr<uint8_t[]> with size BATCH_SIZE
        constexpr size_t BATCH_SIZE = 16; // Using 16 bytes for batch ID
        auto batchId = std::make_unique<uint8_t[]>(BATCH_SIZE);
        
        // 4. Register nodes to the batch
        std::set<reaction::ObserverNode*> nodes = {node1.get(), node2.get()};
        graph.registerActiveBatch<BATCH_SIZE>(batchId.get(), nodes);
        
        // 5. Verify nodes are in batch using ObserverNode::isInActiveBatch
        if (!node1->isInActiveBatch() || !node2->isInActiveBatch()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // 6. Unregister batch
        graph.unregisterActiveBatch(batchId.get());
        
        // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
        if (node1->isInActiveBatch() || node2->isInActiveBatch()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // 8. Return success status
        return true;
        
    } catch (...) {
        // Handle any exceptions and return failure
        return false;
    }
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    graph->addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Observer should have been notified
    }
    
    // 5. Record call count before reset
    int callCountBeforeReset = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // 6. Reset observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // 7. Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after reset
    bool observerNotNotified = !observer->wasChanged() && observer->getCallCount() == callCountBeforeReset;
    
    // 9. Return success status
    return observerNotNotified;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

    void setValue(bool newValue) {
        bool changed = (m_changed != newValue);
        valueChanged(changed);
        m_changed = newValue;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target_node");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set up initial relationship
    observerGraph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify relationship works
    source->setValue(true);
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 5. Reset observer for next test
    target->reset();
    
    // 6. Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool cycleExceptionCaught = false;
    try {
        // 7. Catch cycle exception if thrown
        observerGraph.addObserver(target, source); // This would create a cycle
    } catch (const reaction::CycleException&) {
        cycleExceptionCaught = true;
    }
    
    // 8. Notify target again
    source->setValue(true);
    
    // 9. Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 10. Return success status
    return cycleExceptionCaught && target->wasChanged();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (const auto& observer : observers) {
        observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target->notify(true);
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCallCount = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (const auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCallCount += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return std::make_pair(totalCallCount, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // 1. Create target node
    TestChainedObserver target(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create first observer node
    TestChainedObserver observer1(observer1Name);
    
    // 3. Create second observer node
    TestChainedObserver observer2(observer2Name);
    
    // 4. Get observer graph instance
    reaction::ObserverGraph& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Add target as observer to observer1
    graph.addObserver(observer1, target);
    
    // 6. Add observer1 as observer to observer2
    graph.addObserver(observer2, observer1);
    
    // 7. Reset all change flags
    target.reset();
    observer1.reset();
    observer2.reset();
    
    // 8. Notify target
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    // 9. Check if observer1 was notified
    bool obs1Notified = observer1.wasChanged();
    
    // 10. Check if observer2 was notified  
    bool obs2Notified = observer2.wasChanged();
    
    // 11. Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = observer1.getCallCount() + observer2.getCallCount();
    
    // 12. Chain status: both observers must have been notified
    bool chainStatus = obs1Notified && obs2Notified;
    
    // 13. Return pair with total calls and chain status
    return {totalCalls, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive action with side effects
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param newValue New value to set
 * @return int The value captured by the action (should be newValue after update)
 */
int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to capture side effects
    int capturedValue = 0;
    
    // 3. Create reactive action that updates the variable and captures the value
    auto action = reaction::action([&]() {
        // Update the reactive variable
        reactiveVar.value(newValue);
        
        // Capture the current value
        capturedValue = reactiveVar();
    });
    
    // 4. Execute the action to trigger the update and capture
    action();
    
    // 5. Return captured value
    return capturedValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }
    int getAge() const { return m_age; }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name = name; }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age = age; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // 1. Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    auto person = reaction::var(PersonRecord(initialName, initialAge));
    
    // 2. Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto calcInfo = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return person.getName() + ", " + std::to_string(person.getAge());
    });
    
    // 3. Update name field
    person.setName(newName);

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recompCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        // Increment recomp count each time calculation runs
        recompCount.value(recompCount.get() + 1);
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });
    
    // Force initial computation to establish baseline
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int countAfterNonBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context
    auto batchContext = reaction::batch([&]() {
        // 7. Execute batch - update both variables within batch
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    });
    
    // Execute the batch
    batchContext.execute();
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int countAfterBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    int finalSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return {finalSum, countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. **Creates reactive variables** `a` and `b` using `reaction::var`
2. **Creates a recomp count variable** using `reaction::var(0)`
3. **Creates a calculation** using `reaction::calc` that sums `a` and `b` and increments the recomp count
4. **Performs non-batch updates** by directly setting `a` and `b` values
5. **Records count after non-batch updates** using `recompCount.get()`
6. **Creates batch context** using `reaction::batch`
7. **Executes batch** by calling `execute()` on the batch context
8. **Records count after batch updates** using `recompCount.get()`
9. **Returns final sum and counts** as a tuple

The implementation demonstrates how batch execution reduces recomputation count by grouping multiple updates together, causing the dependent calculation to execute only once instead of multiple times.</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    // Create ReactionException instance with provided parameters
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    // Create ExceptionInfo struct and populate it using ReactionException methods
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();
    info.codeName = reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code);
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate StackMonitor::canAllocate behaviour across modes.
 *
 * @param largeSize Value used for runtime canAllocate after release
 * @return StackAllocationSnapshot Snapshot of allocation checks before/after guard
 */
inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot snapshot;
    
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::setMonitoringEnabled(false);
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // 3. Check allocation without monitoring
    snapshot.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(SBOConfig::CONDITIONAL_SBO_SIZE);
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_SIZE + 1);
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // 4. Enable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::setMonitoringEnabled(true);
    
    // 5. Initialize stack monitor again
    reaction::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // 7. Create StackGuard in scope block
    bool guardSuccess = false;
    bool allocationBlocked = false;
    
    {
        // Using a fixed size for StackGuard since it requires compile-time constant
        constexpr size_t GUARD_SIZE = SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_SIZE + 1;
        reaction::StackMonitor::StackGuard<GUARD_SIZE> guard;
        guardSuccess = guard.success();
        
        // 8. Check if allocation blocked at limit
        reaction::StackMonitor::StackGuard<GUARD_SIZE> secondGuard;
        allocationBlocked = !secondGuard.success();
    }
    
    snapshot.guardSucceeded = guardSuccess;
    snapshot.allocationBlockedAtLimit = allocationBlocked;
    
    // 11. Check runtime allocation after release
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Note:** The implementation uses a fixed size (`SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_SIZE + 1`) for the `StackGuard` since it requires a compile-time constant. instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

        3. Bitwise AND assignment (&=) with a value (say 1) [to clear bits]
